content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

I accept the amendment. The cited test has a real coverage hole: it checks for tmux on the ambient system PATH before installing the fake tmux fixture or prepending the temp bin dir, so on machines without a real tmux binary it returns early and never exercises the new stdout-only exhaustion classification path.

## Votes

- `fr-4-a6c27c9a`: Accept
  The test at tests/unit/tmux_adapter_test.rs:416-423 calls `TmuxAdapter::check_tmux_available()` before `write_fake_tmux(...)` and before `PathGuard::prepend(bin_dir.path())`. `check_tmux_available()` in src/adapters/tmux.rs:133-138 consults `ProcessBackendAdapter::system_path_entries()`, so it only sees the ambient PATH. That means environments without a real tmux installed will silently return from the test and skip the behavior under test at src/adapters/tmux.rs:632-644. This is a genuine regression-test defect because it can report a pass without covering the newly added classification logic. Using `check_tmux_available_in()` against the temp bin dir, or moving fixture setup ahead of the guard, would make the test deterministic.
